rationale: kind=pure-fn over a TYPE refactor: the local lenient interface was replaced by a strict discriminated union from ./types. Any 'mutation' (dropping `lines: []` from a fail callsite, flipping `status: 'ok'` to `'fail'` without lines, etc.) is caught at tsc-noEmit-time — that's the strongest possible mutation gate (rejected at compile, never reaching runtime). L0.types-9 asserts tsc=0 errors; L0.types-10 asserts the import-from-./types invariant. printResult narrowing via 'lines' in r is exhaustively covered by L0.types-2/3/4 (the 3 union variants).
